insight:
Yesterday was a solid recovery day with excellent markers across the board! Your 8.4 hours of sleep and low stress level of 23 really paid off. While 7,619 steps suggests you kept things light with no formal training, your body used this downtime perfectly - that 44 bpm resting heart rate shows you're maintaining fantastic cardiovascular fitness just 9 days out from Chester.

Your HRV of 112ms is sitting right at the top of your baseline range, which is exactly what we want to see in race week. This suggests your nervous system is well-recovered and ready for today's planned session. No need to modify anything - your body is telling us it's primed and ready to handle whatever training we have scheduled.

With such excellent recovery markers, focus on nailing your 175g protein target today to support your final training adaptations. Your metabolism burned 2,387 calories yesterday even on a rest day, so don't skip meals - consistent fueling now sets you up for race day success.
